channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India Tax Deadline: Old vs. New Regime Impacts ELSS, NPS Savings
item.description: As India's fiscal year 2025-26 ends March 31, investors must choose between the old and new tax regimes. The new regime, with lower rates and a higher rebate, reduces ELSS appeal for tax savings, shifting its role to equity investment. NPS, however, keeps strong tax advantages with tiered deductions, making it a robust long-term saving tool. Other old regime deductions for health insurance and donations remain relevant for some.

item.title: RBI Lifts Loan Curbs to Aid India Markets in Turmoil, But Risks Rise
item.description: India's equity markets are experiencing significant declines, fueled by Middle East tensions and rising oil prices, leading to foreign investor outflows. In response, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has eased rules for loans against securities. This aims to give investors needed liquidity and prevent forced asset sales during market volatility, though it introduces risks like margin calls if conditions worsen.

item.title: UCO Bank: Growth Surge Meets Valuation Gap and Global Risks
item.description: UCO Bank posted strong Q3 FY26 results, fueled by 17.2% loan growth and improved asset quality (NPAs at 0.36%). While its price-to-book ratio is low at 1.09x, its Return on Assets (ROA) of 0.83% trails peers like SBI (1.19%) and private banks (1.9-2.1%). Geopolitical instability also adds sector uncertainty, potentially affecting UCO Bank's rural and SME focus.

item.title: Jhunjhunwala's Metro Brands, Jubilant Pharmova Face Valuation, Regulatory Pressure
item.description: Despite Rekha Jhunjhunwala's continued significant holdings, Metro Brands and Jubilant Pharmova face considerable market headwinds. Metro Brands grapples with a premium valuation exceeding industry norms and emerging regulatory compliance issues. Jubilant Pharmova navigates a complex pivot to radiopharmaceuticals, contending with profitability pressures and USFDA scrutiny. Both stocks have seen notable price corrections over the past year, testing investor patience amidst industry-wide shifts.

item.title: India Airport Boom: GMR, L&T Investors Divided on ₹91,000 Cr Plan
item.description: India is channeling substantial investment into airport infrastructure, with Rs 91,000 crore slated for projects. This surge benefits both airport operators like GMR Airports and engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) firms such as Larsen & Toubro, NCC, and Ahluwalia Contracts. However, market perspectives diverge sharply due to varying risk profiles, debt levels, and operational strengths, influencing how investors are valuing these distinct plays on India's aviation growth trajectory.

item.title: India's E20 Ethanol Push: Praj Benefits, Sugar Giants Face Policy Squeeze
item.description: India's drive for 20% ethanol blending in petrol (E20) by April 1, 2026, aims to cut oil imports and emissions. Technology leader Praj Industries is positioned to benefit. However, integrated sugar-ethanol firms like Balrampur Chini Mills and Triveni Engineering face squeezed margins due to higher feedstock costs and flat ethanol prices, with policy dependence a key sector risk.

item.title: India's $72B IPO Share Flood: Valuations Face Test as Lock-Ups Expire
item.description: Around $72 billion in shares from 88 Indian companies are becoming available as IPO lock-in periods expire this week through June 29. This large supply could pressure stock prices. How the market reacts will depend on each company's financial health, its sector, and whether promoters hold onto their shares. Stocks with high valuations or weak financials may see more volatility.

item.title: India Supreme Court: Telecom Spectrum Public Property, Not Insolvency Asset
item.description: India's Supreme Court ruled telecom spectrum is a public resource, not part of a company's assets under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). This decision gives government authority and regulations priority over creditor claims, significantly changing financial expectations for lenders in all sectors that depend on state-granted licenses. The ruling narrows the IBC's scope, highlighting its limits within overall government control.

item.title: Adani Ports Tender Offer Sees Minimal Investor Participation
item.description: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited (APSEZ) has concluded its cash tender offer for USD Senior Notes due 2027 and 2031. Investor participation was much lower than expected, with tendered amounts falling far short of maximum acceptance limits. The company stated the offer was part of its strategy to manage and optimize its debt.

item.title: US Launches 16 Trade Probes, Signals Major Policy Shift
item.description: The U.S. is launching trade investigations in 16 countries, including India, China, and the EU, under Section 301 of the Trade Act. This move comes after a Supreme Court ruling and aims to address overproduction issues in manufacturing. The probes signal a shift in U.S. trade policy that could lead to new tariffs and increased global trade uncertainty.
